company_type: Corporate Payments and Business Expense Management

dependencies: Relationships with major oil companies and petroleum marketers for fuel card acceptance
Partnerships with Mastercard and VISA for third-party payment network processing
Sponsor banks for Mastercard program registration and card issuance
Third-party service providers for technology and network systems
Financial institution counterparties for cross-border derivative contracts
risks: Dependence on relationships with oil companies, fuel merchants, truck stop operators, airlines, and sales channels
Risk of partner bankruptcy or financial distress affecting solution acceptability
Reliance on sponsor banks which could terminate sponsorship or impose less favorable terms
Mastercard could suspend or terminate registration for non-compliance
Third-party service providers may fail to safeguard cardholder data
Counterparty risk in derivative financial instruments for cross-border payments

regulatory_risks: Money transmission and payment instrument licensing regulations across multiple U.S. states
Federal Trade Commission (FTC) lawsuit alleging unfair and deceptive acts and practices
Compliance with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter Terrorist Financing regulations (BSA, USA PATRIOT Act, OFAC sanctions)
Privacy and information security laws (GDPR, CCPA, state data breach notification laws)
Email and text marketing regulations (TCPA, CAN-SPAM Act)
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) supervision and examination
Dodd-Frank Act derivatives regulations and margin requirements
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and U.K. Bribery Act compliance
Interchange fee regulations and potential future reductions
State usury laws limiting interest rates on commercial loans
Escheat laws requiring turnover of unclaimed property
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules (15% rate) being implemented in EU and other jurisdictions
Potential new legislation or regulation on business customers similar to consumer protection laws
Changes in tax laws across global jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions programs administered by OFAC prohibiting transactions with specified countries and nationals
Risk of retaliatory cyber-attacks from nation-state actors due to military and geopolitical conflicts (Russia-Ukraine, Middle East)
Potential supply chain disruptions from geopolitical events
Trade policy changes including tariffs that could increase costs or restrict business operations
Sanctions compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions
